deep_dive.title: Sleep enhances spatial schema memory formation in humans
deep_dive.abstract: Schema memory, a generalized representation formed across episodes sharing regularities, is thought to arise through sleep-dependent systems consolidation. Yet, direct evidence in humans remains sparse. Here, sixty young adults navigated a virtual-reality arena and learned a spatial distribution of object-category ratios across five sessions (toys vs. household items, hidden in boxes at different locations). Participants then either slept or were sleep-deprived for a full night, followed by two recovery nights before memory testing, or they were tested after a short 30-min delay spent awake. Only after a three-day delay did spatial memory of old box locations predict spatial interpolation to new box locations, indicating time-dependent schema expression. Critically, sleep distinctly enhanced spatial integration beyond the effect of time. This benefit was predicted by frontal cortical slow oscillation-spindle coupling during the first post-encoding night, thus linking sleep oscillations to the transformation of episodic spatial memories into integrated schema representations.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Demonstrates sleep-dependent spatial schema memory formation in humans using VR and sleep deprivation
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 通过虚拟现实和睡眠剥夺实验，证明人类空间图式记忆的睡眠依赖性形成
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 通过虚拟现实和睡眠剥夺实验，证明人类空间图式记忆的睡眠依赖性形成
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 通过虚拟现实和睡眠剥夺实验，证明人类空间图式记忆的睡眠依赖性形成
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: In a VR spatial learning task, participants who slept after encoding showed time-dependent schema expression, predicting new locations from old location memory, providing direct evidence for sleep's role in schema memory consolidation.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 本研究让60名年轻人在虚拟现实环境中学习空间物体类别分布，然后进行一整夜睡眠或睡眠剥夺。经过三天延迟后，睡眠组能够基于旧位置记忆推断新位置，表现出时间依赖的图式表达，直接证明了睡眠在抽象图式记忆形成中的关键作用。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 本研究让60名年轻人在虚拟现实环境中学习空间物体类别分布，然后进行一整夜睡眠或睡眠剥夺。经过三天延迟后，睡眠组能够基于旧位置记忆推断新位置，表现出时间依赖的图式表达，直接证明了睡眠在抽象图式记忆形成中的关键作用。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: neurobiological mechanisms of sleep and wakefulness

quick_skim.title: EEGDash: An open-source platform for machine learning on public neurophysiological data
quick_skim.abstract: Public neurophysiological datasets are increasingly accessible but remain hard to reuse: turning one into a trained model still takes thousands of lines of code for download, loading, format repair, windowing, and evaluation, and a dataset that meets metadata standards can still fail to load. EEG-Dash is a software resource that catalogues 791 publicly archived recordings (39,778 participants, over 86,051 hours) spanning electroencephalography (EEG), magnetoencephalography (MEG), intracranial EEG (iEEG), electromyography (EMG), and functional near-infrared spectroscopy (fNIRS) from the OpenNeuro and NEMAR archives. It exposes each dataset as an importable, queryable class that preserves signal attributes and loads into machine-learning workflows without custom code, delegating signal handling to MNE-Python, windowing to Braindecode, and format compliance to the official Brain Imaging Data Structure (BIDS) validator. A metadata-first registry adds semantic search, a format-repair layer, automatic dataset-level tags drawn from each source publication, and a feature-extraction framework. The catalogue, with per-record loadability and compliance metadata, supports benchmarking, model development, and cross-dataset analysis.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Open-source platform for neurophysiological data, enabling ML on EEG/MEG/iEEG for sleep research
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 开源神经生理数据平台，支持睡眠研究中的机器学习
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 开源神经生理数据平台，支持睡眠研究中的机器学习
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 开源神经生理数据平台，支持睡眠研究中的机器学习
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: EEG-Dash is an open-source platform that catalogs 791 neurophysiological recordings, providing importable, queryable classes for machine learning on EEG, MEG, iEEG, EMG, and fNIRS data, removing data engineering barriers and directly enabling sleep neuroscience studies.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: EEG-Dash是一个开源平台，整合了来自OpenNeuro和NEMAR的791个记录，提供统一接口，支持EEG、MEG、iEEG等多种模态数据的机器学习。它通过消除繁琐的数据加载和预处理步骤，使研究人员能够快速将公开神经生理数据用于模型训练与评估，对于睡眠神经科学领域尤其有价值，因为该领域常依赖大规模EEG数据集。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: EEG-Dash是一个开源平台，整合了来自OpenNeuro和NEMAR的791个记录，提供统一接口，支持EEG、MEG、iEEG等多种模态数据的机器学习。它通过消除繁琐的数据加载和预处理步骤，使研究人员能够快速将公开神经生理数据用于模型训练与评估，对于睡眠神经科学领域尤其有价值，因为该领域常依赖大规模EEG数据集。

quick_skim.title: BAYESIAN STATE-SPACE MODEL FOR JOINT INFERENCE OF OSCILLATORY DYNAMICS AND POINT-PROCESS COUPLING
quick_skim.abstract: Under a range of behavioral and physiological conditions, spike times and local field potential (LFP) oscillations exhibit phase coupling within specific frequency bands. Classical measures such as spike--field coherence (SFC) and the phase-locking value (PLV) quantify this coupling but estimate the LFP spectrum independently of spike timing. We introduce Joint SSMT, a Bayesian state-space framework that jointly infers LFP spectrograms and spike--field coupling strength. The model treats narrowband LFP activity as a latent process evolving in continuous time, with spike trains linked to the complex spectral state through a Bernoulli--logistic model. In simulations, Joint SSMT accurately recovers coupling strength, denoises the spectrogram, and uses spike timing to resolve fine temporal structure in the LFP. Applied to propofol anesthesia data, the model identifies coupling at a specific slow-oscillation frequency where SFC and PLV report only broad low-frequency coupling. We extend Joint SSMT to trial-structured experiments and apply it to primate recordings during an associative learning task, revealing frequency-specific coupling in hippocampus and prefrontal cortex. We also derive closed-form expressions for SFC and PLV as functions of the generative model parameters. Across simulations and two primate datasets, Joint SSMT provides more frequency-specific coupling estimates with principled uncertainty quantification than classical PLV and SFC.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 神经振荡状态空间模型睡眠
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 神经振荡状态空间模型睡眠
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 神经振荡状态空间模型睡眠
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Introduces a Bayesian state-space model (Joint SSMT) that jointly infers LFP spectrograms and spike-field coupling, improving accuracy over classical methods.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文提出了一种贝叶斯状态空间模型Joint SSMT，能够联合推断局部场电位频谱图和锋电位-场耦合强度。该模型将窄带LFP活动视为连续时间潜在过程，通过伯努利-逻辑斯蒂模型连接锋电位序列。模拟实验表明，Joint SSMT准确恢复了耦合强度并去除了噪声，为神经振荡分析提供了更强大的统计框架。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文提出了一种贝叶斯状态空间模型Joint SSMT，能够联合推断局部场电位频谱图和锋电位-场耦合强度。该模型将窄带LFP活动视为连续时间潜在过程，通过伯努利-逻辑斯蒂模型连接锋电位序列。模拟实验表明，Joint SSMT准确恢复了耦合强度并去除了噪声，为神经振荡分析提供了更强大的统计框架。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: Papers central to 研究睡眠神经科学领域的学术文章, especially work that connects or combines: advancements in sleep neuroscience research; neurobiological mechanisms of sleep and wakefulness; neural regulation of the sleep-wake cycle; brain circuits involved in sleep control; neural mechanisms of rapid eye movement sleep; neurobiology of non-rapid eye movement sleep; recent advances in sleep neuroscience research; neural mechanisms underlying sleep wake regulation; neurobiological basis of clinical sleep disorders.
